| First game        = {{fb|CHA}} 2–2 {{fb-rt|NIG}} <br /> ([[Abidjan]], [[Ivory Coast]]; 25 December 1961)
| Largest win       = {{nowrap|{{fb|CHA}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|STP}} <br /> ([[Libreville]], [[Gabon]]; June 29, 1976) <br /> {{fb|CHA}} 5–0 {{fb-rt|STP}} <br /> ([[Libreville]], [[Gabon]]; 10 November 1999)}}
| Largest loss      = {{fb|CGO}} 11–0 {{fb-rt|CHA}} <br /> ([[Republic of the Congo|Congo]]; 28 March 1964)
}}
The '''Chad national football team''' ({{langx|ar|منتخب تشاد لكرة القدم}}, {{langx|fr|Équipe du Tchad de football}}), nicknamed '''''Les Sao''''' ({{langx|ar|ساو}}),  represents [[Chad]] in international [[Association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Chadian Football Federation]], the governing body for football in the country. The team has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]] finals or [[Africa Cup of Nations]]. Home matches are played at the [[:fr:Stade Olympique Maréchal Idriss Déby Itno|Maréchal Idriss Déby Itno Olympic Stadium]].<ref name="auto">{{cite web|url=http://www.alwihdainfo.com/Tchad-La-liste-des-22-joueurs-retenus-pour-le-match-contre-le-Nigeria_a17064.html|title=Tchad : La liste des 22 joueurs retenus pour le match contre le Nigeria|first=Mahamat|last=Ramadane|website=Alwihdainfo|access-date=2015-06-09|archive-date=2015-06-09|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150609150503/http://www.alwihdainfo.com/Tchad-La-liste-des-22-joueurs-retenus-pour-le-match-contre-le-Nigeria_a17064.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{NFT player|id=38881|name=Léger Djimrangar|accessdate= }}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://tchadinfos.com/societes/ligue-champions-quipes-tchadiennes-limines/|title=Ligue des champions : les équipes tchadiennes éliminées|date=1 March 2015|website=Tchadinfos.com|access-date=17 June 2015|archive-date=2 September 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150902221106/http://tchadinfos.com/societes/ligue-champions-quipes-tchadiennes-limines/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.national-football-teams.com/player/1385/Marius_Mbaiam.html|title=Marius Mbaiam|first=Benjamin|last=Strack-Zimmermann|website=National-football-teams.com|access-date=2015-01-18|archive-date=2015-09-06|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150906112033/http://www.national-football-teams.com/player/1385/Marius_Mbaiam.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldfootball/statisticsandrecords/players/player=298523/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080627001810/http://www.fifa.com/worldfootball/statisticsandrecords/players/player=298523/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=June 27, 2008|title=FIFA Tournaments - Players & Coaches - Karl Marx Barthélémy|website=FIFA.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.goal.com/id/berita/semen-padang-resmi-kontrak-striker-asal-chad/1tcc0velepukm18v139ndi2i04|title=Semen Padang Resmi Kontrak Striker Asal Chad|website=goal.com|access-date=2019-03-29|archive-date=2019-03-29|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190329234231/https://www.goal.com/id/berita/semen-padang-resmi-kontrak-striker-asal-chad/1tcc0velepukm18v139ndi2i04|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.national-football-teams.com/player/24388/Ezechiel_Ndouassel.html|title=Ezechiel Ndouassel|first=Benjamin|last=Strack-Zimmermann|website=National-football-teams.com|access-date=2015-01-18|archive-date=2020-11-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201101224657/https://www.national-football-teams.com/player/24388/Ezechiel_Ndouassel.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.national-football-teams.com/player/45683/Rodrigue_Ninga.html|title=Casimir Ninga|first=Benjamin|last=Strack-Zimmermann|website=National-football-teams.com|access-date=2015-01-18|archive-date=2018-02-24|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180224042844/http://www.national-football-teams.com/player/45683/Rodrigue_Ninga.html|url-status=live}}</ref> In April 2021, [[FIFA]] banned the Chad national football team from participating in global football over the Chadian government's interference with the Chadian Football Federation.<ref name=":0">{{Cite news|title=Fifa bans Chad from global football|language=en-GB|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/africa/56661109|access-date=2021-04-07|archive-date=2021-04-07|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210407092131/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/africa/56661109|url-status=live}}</ref>

==History==
The [[Chadian Football Federation]] was founded in 1962 and became affiliated to [[FIFA]] and [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] in 1988.

Chad's national football team did not play a big role in world football until the 1990s. They did not enter any qualifiers for Continental or World Cups until the early 1990s when they played their first qualifying matches for the [[1992 Africa Cup of Nations]], hosted by [[Senegal]].<ref>{{cite web|title=World Football Elo Ratings: Chad|url=http://www.eloratings.net/Chad.htm|website=Eloratings.net|access-date=10 November 2010|archive-date=22 November 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101122055355/http://eloratings.net/Chad.htm|url-status=live}}</ref> Until then, they played mostly friendly matches and minor cups, mostly with African teams.

The national team had a difficult start − the first match they ever played was in the [[L'Amitié Tournament]], which took place in [[Senegal]] in 1963, and it was a 2–1 defeat to [[Liberia national football team|Liberia]]. Soon after, they suffered a 6–2 loss to [[Dahomey]] (now [[Benin]]).

The Chad national football team never qualified for either the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] or the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]]. Their first participation in the World Cup qualifiers was in [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2000]] when they were eliminated in the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF first round|first round]] by [[Liberia national football team|Liberia]], losing 0–1 at home, and playing 0–0 away. They were coached by Marcel Mao. Their next attempt was in [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|2003]], under Yann Djim, but they were eliminated by [[Angola national football team|Angola]]. They won their first home game when [[Francis Oumar Belonga]] scored a hat trick, 3–1. They lost their away match 2–0, and went no further. The [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|qualifiers]] for the [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010 World Cup]] brought more matches because they played in a group phase together with [[Mali national football team|Mali]], [[Sudan national football team|Sudan]] and [[Congo national football team|Congo]]. They finished at the bottom of the group, with six points (two wins and four losses).

In [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CAF)|the qualifying competition]] for the [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014 World Cup]], Chad faced [[Tanzania national football team|Tanzania]] in a first-round playoff. Tanzania advanced despite shockingly losing 1–0 at home at [[Dar es Salaam]]'s National Stadium. The Taifa Stars were tipped for an emphatic victory over the visitors, having won the first-leg 2–1 in [[N'Djamena]]. Chad stunned the home side three minutes after the break when Mahamat Ahmat Labo struck to silence the home crowd. But Tanzania managed to progress to the next round thanks to the away goals rule as they were tied 2–2 on aggregate.

Chad played against [[Malawi national football team|Malawi]] in the [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|qualifiers]] for the [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations]]. Chad won the first leg match 3–2, but lost 2–0 in [[Blantyre]] to be eliminated with an aggregate score of 4–3.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.nyasatimes.com/malawi/2012/06/16/malawi-rallies-to-beat-chad-in-africa-cup-of-nations-qualifiers/|title=Malawi rallies to beat Chad in Africa Cup of Nations qualifiers|newspaper=Malawi Nyasa Times|access-date=2012-06-24|archive-date=2012-06-22|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120622050400/http://www.nyasatimes.com/malawi/2012/06/16/malawi-rallies-to-beat-chad-in-africa-cup-of-nations-qualifiers/|url-status=live}}</ref>

In the [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|qualifiers]] for the [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations]], Malawi was the opponent once again. Chad lost 2–0 in the first leg at Kamuzu Stadium in Blantyre, but they won 3–1 in at Idriss Mahamat Ouya Stadium in N'Djamena, losing on the away goals rule. Forward [[Robin Ngalande]] turned into a savior for Malawi when he came off the bench to score a crucial late goal.

The biggest achievement for Chad so far is a [[CEMAC Cup]] (Economic and Monetary Community of Central Africa) title in 2014, after beating [[Congo national football team|Congo]] 3–2 in the final on December 14 in [[Bata, Equatorial Guinea|Bata]], [[Equatorial Guinea]]. French-born [[Emmanuel Trégoat]] managed the team.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sofoot.com/emmanuel-tregoat-on-s-est-fait-cambrioler-notre-vestiaire-a-la-mi-temps-a-domicile-198125.html|title=Emmanuel Tregoat : " on s'est fait cambrioler notre vestiaire à la mi-temps, à domicile "|date=25 March 2015 |access-date=2019-11-14|archive-date=2020-07-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200714000516/https://www.sofoot.com/emmanuel-tregoat-on-s-est-fait-cambrioler-notre-vestiaire-a-la-mi-temps-a-domicile-198125.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Their previous best was a second place in the 2005 edition, when they lost to [[Cameroon national football team|Cameroon]] in the final.<ref>{{cite web| url = https://allafrica.com/stories/201412160261.html| url-status = dead| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20141216180543/http://allafrica.com/stories/201412160261.html| archive-date = 2014-12-16| title = allAfrica.com: Chad Emerge Winners of 2014 Cemac Cup}}</ref>

''Les Sao'' had a disappointing start into their [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|qualifying campaign]] for the [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations]], losing 2–0 to [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]] and slumping to a 5–1 defeat at home against [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]]. But it was then that things took a turn for the better, as coach [[Moudou Kouta]], who was in charge of the side on an interim basis, took the team to an unexpected victory against [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]] in the first round of the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification|qualifiers]] for the [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018 World Cup]]. ''Les Sao'' won 1–0<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/matches/round=276416/match=300335110/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150906051429/http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/matches/round=276416/match=300335110/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=September 6, 2015|title=2018 FIFA World Cup Russia - Matches - Chad-Sierra Leone |website=FIFA.com}}</ref> at home and even though they were beaten 2–1<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.fifa.com/worldcup/matches/round=276416/match=300337138/index.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150923183444/http://www.fifa.com/worldcup/matches/round=276416/match=300337138/index.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=September 23, 2015|title=2018 FIFA World Cup Russia - Matches - Sierra Leone-Chad |website=FIFA.com}}</ref> in the return leg, they advanced to the second round of the qualifiers on away goals. Chad sensationally defeated [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]] 1–0 at home in the second round before falling to a 4–0 defeat in [[Alexandria]] three days later.

In March 2016, the Chadian Football Federation announced they were withdrawing from the 2017 Africa Cup of Nations qualification due to financial problems. The team was subsequently banned from entering the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|following edition]], meaning they would play no official games for over three years until September 2019, when Chad lost 1–3 at home to [[Sudan national football team|Sudan]] in
[[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification]]. A month later ''Les Sao'' defeated [[Liberia national football team|Liberia]] on penalties to reach the Group Stage of [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]].

In March 2021, the Chadian government dissolved the [[Chadian Football Federation]], leaving Chad facing a likely ban under FIFA regulations that prevent governments from interfering in the running of a country's football team.<ref>{{cite web |title=Chad FA disbanded, facing Fifa ban |url=https://www.goal.com/en/news/chad-fa-disbanded-facing-fifa-ban/1fp3ibbss7h6015vtgklmma1t5 |website=Goal.com |access-date=16 March 2021 |archive-date=15 March 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210315235243/https://www.goal.com/en/news/chad-fa-disbanded-facing-fifa-ban/1fp3ibbss7h6015vtgklmma1t5 |url-status=live }}</ref> In April 2021, FIFA announced an indefinite ban from global football, citing the Chadian government's interference.<ref name=":0" /> Due to this decision, [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] disqualified Chad from its participation in the [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations qualification Group A|2021 AFCON qualifiers]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Political meddling gets Chad kicked out of AFCON qualifiers |url=https://panafricanfootball.com/2021/03/23/political-meddling-gets-chad-kicked-out-of-afcon-qualifiers/ |website=panafricanfootball.com |access-date=23 March 2021 |archive-date=23 March 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210323201931/https://panafricanfootball.com/2021/03/23/political-meddling-gets-chad-kicked-out-of-afcon-qualifiers/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

== Kits ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Kit provider
! Period
|-
| {{flagicon|CHA}} Salamant
| 1974–1979
|-
| {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Mitre Sports International|Mitre]]
| 1980–1992
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Fila (company)|Fila]]
| 1993–2000
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Adidas]]
| 2001–2020
|-
| {{flagicon|FRA}} [[Decathlon (retailer)|Kipsta]]
| 2020–2021
|-
| {{flagicon|CHN}} [[Anta Sports|Anta]]
| 2021–2022
|-
| {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Macron (sportswear)|Macron]]
| 2022–2024
|-
| {{flagicon|GER}} [[Puma (brand)|Puma]]
| 2024–present 
|}

During the independence of Chad, the team competed in international matches and played in a blue shirt, blue shorts, and red socks. These are the national colours of the [[France|French]] flag. In the 1970s, Chad's home colors changed to blue-yellow-red. Their first away kit in 1960 was white shirt, red shorts, and white socks. In 1980, the away kit color changed into red shirt, blue shorts, and yellow socks.{{citation needed|date=July 2025}}

== Results and fixtures ==
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.{{citation needed|date=July 2025}}

===Coaching history===
{{Div col|colwidth=20em}}
*{{flagicon|Soviet Union}} [[Vasily Sokolov (footballer)|Vasily Sokolov]] (1967–1971)
*{{flagicon|Soviet Union}} [[Anzor Kavazashvili]] (1976–1977)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Yakia Aoudou]] (1985)<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/coach/93975/Yakia_Aoudou.html | title=Yakia Aoudou (Coach) }}</ref>
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Moussarou Ngongolo]] (1998)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.11v11.com/matches/chad-v-congo-02-august-1998-266242/|title=Chad v Congo, 02 August 1998|website=11v11.com|access-date=22 June 2015|archive-date=22 June 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150622160813/http://www.11v11.com/matches/chad-v-congo-02-august-1998-266242/|url-status=live}}</ref> 
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Yann Djim Ngarlendana]] (1998)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.11v11.com/matches/congo-v-chad-16-august-1998-265700/|title=Congo v Chad, 16 August 1998|website=11v11.com|access-date=22 June 2015|archive-date=22 June 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150622164032/http://www.11v11.com/matches/congo-v-chad-16-august-1998-265700/|url-status=live}}</ref> 
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Douba Djibrine]] (1999)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1999af.html|title=International Matches 1999 - Africa|website=Rsssf.com|access-date=2023-02-02|archive-date=2023-04-01|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230401163953/https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1999af.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
*{{Flagicon|France|1974}} [[Marcel Mao]] (2000)
*{{Flagicon|Cameroon}} [[Jean Paul Akono]] (2002–2003)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Yann Djim Ngarlendana]] (2003)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Yann Djim Ngarlendana]] (2005–2006)<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cemac05.html|title=Coupe de la CEMAC 2005|website=Rsssf.com|access-date=2023-02-02|archive-date=2022-11-29|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221129094146/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cemac05.html|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.news24.com/Columnists/Archive/TumoMokone/Do-your-homework-Bafana-20060302|title=Do your homework Bafana|website=News24.com|access-date=2013-04-07|archive-date=2014-07-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140714133857/http://www.news24.com/Columnists/Archive/TumoMokone/Do-your-homework-Bafana-20060302|url-status=dead}}</ref>
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Moudou Kouta]] (2006)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Natoltiga Okalah]] (2006–2007)<ref name=autogenerated1>{{cite web|url=http://www.camfoot.com/?coupe-cemac-2007-les-saos-domptent,8108.html|title=Coupe Cemac 2007 : Les Saos domptent les Lions A' 2 à 1|date=5 March 2007|website=Camfoot}}</ref>
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Mahamat Adoum]] (2007)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.lusakatimes.com/2007/06/16/chad-leave-zambia-in-a-dust-storm/|title=Zambia : Chad leave Zambia in a dust storm|date=16 June 2007|website=Lusaka Times|access-date=9 April 2012|archive-date=12 June 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140612040920/http://www.lusakatimes.com/2007/06/16/chad-leave-zambia-in-a-dust-storm/|url-status=live}}</ref>
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Natoltiga Okalah]] (2008)
*{{Flagicon|Egypt}} [[Sherif El-Khashab]] (2009–2011)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Moudou Kouta]] (2011–2013)<ref>{{cite web|url=http://tchadsport.centerblog.net/17.html|title=TCHADSPORT - Page 17|website=Tchadsport.centerblog.net|access-date=2013-04-07|archive-date=2013-07-03|archive-url=https://archive.today/20130703055653/http://tchadsport.centerblog.net/17.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
*{{Flagicon|France|1974}} [[Emmanuel Trégoat]] (2014–2015)<ref name="centerblog.net">{{cite web|url=http://tchadsport.centerblog.net/|title=TCHADSPORT|website=Tchadsport.centerblog.net|access-date=2010-07-18|archive-date=2012-07-19|archive-url=https://archive.today/20120719023348/http://tchadsport.centerblog.net/|url-status=live}}</ref> 
*{{Flagicon|Cameroon}} [[Rigobert Song]] (2015)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Moudou Kouta]] (2015–2016)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Djimtan Yatamadji]] (2019)
*{{Flagicon|France|1974}} [[Emmanuel Trégoat]] (2019–2020)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Djimtan Yatamadji]] (2020–2023)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Kévin Nicaise]] (2023–2025)
*{{Flagicon|Chad}} [[Tahir Gardia]] (2025–present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players have been called up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group I]] matches against [[Mali national football team|Mali]] and [[Central African Republic national football team|Central African Republic]] on 8 and 12 October 2025; respectively.<ref>{{cite web |title=Le sélectionneur national a dévoilé la liste des joueurs retenus pour les deux rencontres des Sao face au Mali (8 octobre) et à la RCA (12 octobre), au Stade Olympique Idriss Deby Itno |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1761822131431193&set=a.962121944734553&locale=en_GB |website=Facebook |publisher=Fédération Tchadienne de Football Association |date=2 October 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals current as of 8 October 2025, after the match against [[Mali national football team|Mali]].''

==Player records==

:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Chad.''

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[CEMAC Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': [[2014 CEMAC Cup|2014]]
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): 2015
* '''[[UDEAC Championship]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (2): 1986, 1987
